investor_outreach: Find one investor who ghosted you. Follow up today.
DM an investor who liked a startup post similar to yours.
Ask one founder who just raised how they did it.
Write one line that explains your startup without buzzwords.
Send a cold email that only asks for feedback, not funding.
Reply to an old rejection and keep the door open.
Shortlist three funds that actually fit your stage.
Find a mutual LinkedIn connection to intro you to an investor.
Turn your last investor update into a tweet.
Reach out to one angel investor you’ve been overthinking about.
sales: Find three companies that need you but don’t know it yet.
Write one cold email that sounds human.
Follow up with a lead who ghosted you two weeks ago.
Record a 15-second video pitch. Send it.
Ask one happy client for a referral today.
Turn a rejection into a testimonial request.
Send a five-word cold email subject line.
Rephrase your pitch to start with their pain, not your product.
Follow up by giving value, not asking again.
DM one potential client directly. Skip the formality.
job_applicants: Message one hiring manager instead of applying online.
Reach out to an alum working at your dream company.
Apply to one job you think you’re underqualified for.
Send a follow-up email for an application you haven’t heard back on.
DM a company founder about why you want to work there.
Turn your last rejection email into a connection request.
Find one person in your dream role and ask for advice.
Rewrite your CV headline to say what you actually want next.
Research three people who got your dream role. What did they do?
Send one job email that doesn’t mention your resume.
scholarships: Reach out to a past recipient and ask what made their essay stand out.
Write the first 100 words of your personal statement. No edits.
DM a current scholar and ask what surprised them most.
Edit one paragraph to show more results than dreams.
Follow up with a referee who hasn’t submitted your letter yet.
Find two scholarships you qualify for but haven’t applied to.
Ask one person to proofread your essay for tone, not grammar.
Write one sentence that answers: why you, why now?
Turn your essay’s opening into something a reviewer would remember.
collaboration: Message one person whose work truly aligns with yours.
Ask one person to collaborate on something small this week.
Follow up with someone who said 'maybe later.'
Draft a DM to someone you admire — and send it.
Pitch a joint post or idea to a peer you respect.
Comment meaningfully on three posts from people you want to work with.
Email a journalist who’s covered your field recently.
Reach out to a podcast host whose audience fits your work.
Ask a friend to intro you to one potential collaborator.
Send one cold message that doesn’t ask for anything — just shares value.
